info.title: HIV.D Care-Treatment API
info.description: This tab describes the data that may be collected during care and treatment clinical visit for HIV workflow (HIV.D)

get.summary: JSON Schema definition for the Logical Model StructureDefinition-HIVDCareTreatment
get.description: This endpoint serves the JSON Schema definition for the Logical Model StructureDefinition-HIVDCareTreatment.

StructureDefinition-HIVDCareTreatment.title: HIV.D Care-Treatment
StructureDefinition-HIVDCareTreatment.description: This tab describes the data that may be collected during care and treatment clinical visit for HIV workflow (HIV.D)

resourceType.description: Resource type identifier for HIVDCareTreatment logical model

@context.description: JSON-LD context for this logical model with ValueSet vocabularies

anyOf.description: URI reference to external JSON-LD context

@type.description: JSON-LD type identifier for HIVDCareTreatment logical model
JSON-LD type reference to ValueSet HIV.D.DE1

@id.description: Full IRI of the code from ValueSet HIV.D.DE1

items.description: Coding from ValueSet HIV.D.DE1. Supports both FHIR Coding structure and JSON-LD structure.

reasonForVisit.description: Whether visit was scheduled or unscheduled, clinical only, or for ARV drug pick-up

scheduledVisit.description: Is this is a scheduled visit?

bodyTemperature.description: Temperature of the client in Celsius

respiratoryRate.description: The number of breaths per minute

heartRate.description: The number of heartbeats per minute

bodyWeight.description: The client's current weight in kilograms

bloodPressureCannotBeTaken.description: If the client's blood pressure cannot be taken, this should be indicated here. Otherwise, blood pressure should be measured.

@type.description: JSON-LD type reference to ValueSet HIV.D.DE17

@id.description: Full IRI of the code from ValueSet HIV.D.DE17

items.description: Coding from ValueSet HIV.D.DE17. Supports both FHIR Coding structure and JSON-LD structure.

signsOfSeriousIllness.description: Signs that may indicate the client has a serious illness and needs triage or an emergency referral

otherSignOfSeriousIllnessSpecify.description: Client is exhibiting another sign of a serious illness (specify)

breastfeeding.description: Client is giving infant breast milk

numberOfPregnanciesGravida.description: Total number of times the woman has been pregnant (gravida)

numberOfMiscarriagesAndOrAbortions.description: Total number of pregnancies lost/ended due to miscarriages and/or abortions before 22 weeks/5 months

numberOfLiveBirths.description: Total number of live births after 22 weeks

parity.description: Calculated parity is the total number of live and stillbirths

serodiscordantPartner.description: Client's HIV status is different from a current partner's HIV status

artStartDate.description: The date on which the client started or restarted ART

dateArtStopped.description: Date on which client stopped ART

establishedOnArt.description: Is the client successfully established on ART?

@type.description: JSON-LD type reference to ValueSet HIV.D.DE43

@id.description: Full IRI of the code from ValueSet HIV.D.DE43

artStartType.description: Whether the client is ART naive or is restarting ART

dateOfInitiationOnArt.description: The date on which the client was first initiated on ART

timeOnArt.description: Time the client has been on ART since starting or restarting it in years and months

datesArtRestarted.description: Date(s) client restarted ART after stopping (intentionally interrupting) for any number of reasons (see 'Reason ART stopped')

artCohort.description: Month and year client originally started ART (documented) at a health facility in the system. The cohort is a group of patients who started ART in the same month (or quarter) and year, whose status is followed over time, using the ART register.

transferInForHivCare.description: Client is transferring in with records or known ART drugs and ART start date

dateOfTransferIn.description: Date client presented at facility (with transfer/referral slip) from another facility (and on ART) within the system

@type.description: JSON-LD type reference to ValueSet HIV.D.DE52

@id.description: Full IRI of the code from ValueSet HIV.D.DE52

facilityTransferredFrom.description: Name of health facility client was transferred from

dateEnrolledInHivCare.description: Date client first enrols in HIV care at the facility. Begins when a person with a confirmed HIV diagnosis presents to a facility where HIV care is provided and a medical record, patient card, file or chart is opened for the first time. This could be at an HIV care/ART, MNCH or TB clinic.

ageAtEnrolment.description: Client's age when the client was enrolled in ART care

@type.description: JSON-LD type reference to ValueSet HIV.D.DE55

@id.description: Full IRI of the code from ValueSet HIV.D.DE55

facilityWhereClientFirstEnrolledInHivCar.description: Facility where the client first enrolled in HIV care

hivClinicalStage.description: WHO clinical stage of client based on signs and symptoms. WHO clinical staging is a way to categorize HIV disease severity based on new or recurrent clinical events. There are 4 WHO clinical stages that range from mild symptoms (WHO clinical stage 1) to severe symptoms (WHO clinical stage 4).

numberOfMissedDoses.description: Number of doses of antiretroviral therapy (ART) the client missed since the last visit, used for monitoring adherence

receivedViralLoadTestResult.description: Client received results from viral load test

dateViralLoadTestResultsReceivedByClient.description: The date on which the client received results from viral load test

dateOfViralLoadSampleCollection.description: Date and time when the sample was collected to test the client's HIV viral load

dateOfScheduledReviewOfViralLoadTestResu.description: Expected date when client's viral load test results will be returned and reviewed

dateOfArtInterruption.description: Date of client's ART interruption (ART stop or missed drug pick-up)

typeOfTreatmentLimitingToxicity.description: Type of treatment-limiting toxicity experienced by client. Treatment-limiting toxicity is defined as a serious adverse drug reaction that results in drug discontinuation or substitution. In addition, any reaction that leads to treatment interruption or requires changing the drug or regimen because of an adverse drug reaction is also considered a serious adverse drug reaction.

unexpectedAdverseDrugReactionSpecify.description: Specify the type of unexpected adverse drug reaction the client experienced

datesOfSubstitutionWithinFirstLineRegime.description: Date on which ARV drug regimen (one or more drugs) for client was changed within the first-line regimen (substitution)
